---
title: "Maintainer notes"
subtitle: "Data formats, qs migration, and lesson impact (staff only)"
draft: true
---

**Audience:** Tim, course staff, and repo maintainers — not students.

**Students** use `readRDS()` / `saveRDS()` in labs 3–4 and do not need the package-ecosystem detail below. This page documents *why* we changed the 2026 materials and how to talk about serialization in a reproducible pipeline.

---

## Summary (Summer 2026)

| Item | Before | After (2026 DataHub) |
|------|--------|----------------------|
| Eviction file | `data/evictions/d5_case_aggregated.qs` | **`.qs2`** (primary) + **`.rds`** (fallback) |
| Lab 3–4 load | `qread(...)` | `read_eviction_data()` → `qs2::qs_read` or `readRDS` |
| Lab 3 student save | `qsave(...)` | `saveRDS(...)` (+ qs2 noted in format comments) |
| Course dependency | `qs` in install script | **`qs2`** (+ RDS fallback; no `qs`) |
| Legacy `.qs` in repo | — | Provenance; regenerate via `code/convert_eviction_data.R` |

**Trigger:** [`qs` was archived from CRAN on 2026-01-17](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/qs/index.html) and **failed to compile** on Berkeley r.datahub even from the [CRAN Archive](https://cran.r-project.org/src/contrib/Archive/qs/). Students could not complete labs 3–4.

---

## What is RDS?

**RDS** (R Data Serialization) is R’s built-in binary format for saving a single R object to disk.

```r
saveRDS(my_object, "my_object.rds")
my_object <- readRDS("my_object.rds")
```

- **Ships with base R** — no CRAN dependency, works on DataHub, Binder, and collaborators’ laptops without extra setup.
- **Uses R’s native `serialize()` / `unserialize()` pipeline** — the same mechanism R has used for decades.
- **Trade-off:** Slower and larger than compressed serializers (`qs`, `qs2`, `fst`) on very large objects, but **maximum portability** for teaching and long-term reproducibility.

For course-scale objects (~7 MB RDS for Indiana evictions), RDS is fast enough on DataHub.

---

## Format decision deep dive (is RDS ideal for this data?)

### What the eviction file actually is

Profiled from `d5_case_aggregated.rds` (June 2026):

| Property | Value |
|----------|-------|
| Structure | Plain `tibble` / `data.frame` — **not** an `sf` object (no geometry column) |
| Rows × cols | 139,072 × 58 |
| Grain | Tract × month (2016–2022), Indiana |
| Key join keys | `tract_geoid`, `county`, `year`, `plot_date` |
| Main analysis cols | `filings`, `tenants`, tract/county renter denominators (`tr_totrent`, …) |
| In-memory size | ~61 MB |
| On-disk sizes (measured) | **RDS 6.7 MB** · legacy qs 10.2 MB · gzip CSV 5.5 MB · Parquet 7.8 MB |

So this is a **wide analytic table** with repeated tract identifiers (1,696 unique tracts × many months), not a spatial file. Maps in lab 4 join it to `tigris` tract boundaries by `GEOID` / `tract_geoid`.

### Two different jobs: “cache” vs “interchange”

| Job | Question | Best formats | Why |
|-----|----------|--------------|-----|
| **Pipeline cache** | “I’m in R, I want to reload this exact object fast tomorrow without re-querying APIs.” | **RDS**, qs2, (legacy qs) | Preserves R types (`Date`, `integer` vs `numeric`), single-object round-trip, no extra packages for RDS |
| **Interchange** | “Someone in Python / Excel / QGIS / Datawrapper needs this file.” | **CSV**, **Parquet**, GeoPackage (if spatial) | Tool-agnostic; human-readable (CSV); columnar + typed cross-language (Parquet) |

**Quick serialization** (RDS, qs2) optimizes the **cache** job.  
**Interchange formats** optimize **sharing and longevity outside one runtime**.

Confusing the two is a common reproducibility mistake — e.g. publishing only `.rds` to GitHub when a policy partner needs CSV, or caching only CSV and losing `Date` types between sessions.

### Cross-language options (if not opening in R)

| Format | Python | Excel | GIS | Preserve types | Our file size | Course fit |
|--------|--------|-------|-----|----------------|---------------|------------|
| **RDS** | No (without rpy2 etc.) | No | No | Full R types | 6.7 MB | **Best for R-only labs** |
| **CSV / CSV.gz** | Excellent | Excellent | OK (table join) | Weak (`Date` → character) | 5.5 MB gz | Already taught lab 3 |
| **Parquet** | Excellent (pandas, polars) | Limited | Via GDAL | Strong | 7.8 MB | Good **ERN distribution** format; needs `arrow` in R to teach |
| **qs2** | No | No | No | R types | ~similar to RDS | Extra package; no gain at 7 MB |
| **GeoPackage** | Via geopandas | No | Excellent | Spatial | N/A | **Wrong tool** — data has no geometry |

**Parquet is the serious cross-language choice** for analytic tables (ERN → Python pipelines, DuckDB, etc.). For *this* dataset at *this* size, Parquet is not smaller than RDS — so the reason to add it would be **interoperability**, not compression.

### Recommendation for SOC-N100

| Layer | Format | Action |
|-------|--------|--------|
| **Student labs (canonical)** | `.qs2` + `qs2` | **Primary** — teaches CRAN successor; `read_eviction_data()` falls back to `.rds` |
| **Fallback / resilience** | `.rds` | If `qs2` compile fails on a hub |
| **Legacy provenance** | `.qs` | Keep in repo; maintainer conversion only. |
| **Optional ERN / Python mirror** | `.csv.gz` or `.parquet` | **Optional** — add only if Tim wants a non-R distribution path; do not replace RDS in labs unless willing to add `arrow` to `install_course_packages.R`. |
| **Do not use in course** | qs, qs2 for this file | Archived / extra compile deps; no pedagogical win at 7 MB. |

**Bottom line:** RDS is **ideal for the teaching context** (R on DataHub). It is **not** the ideal **public interchange** format if ERN wants Python collaborators — that would be **documented Parquet or CSV** alongside RDS, not instead of it in labs.

### When quick serialization vs interchange matters *for this data*

- **Indiana evictions (course handout):** Loaded once per lab session, 7 MB — RDS load time is negligible. qs2 would save perhaps a second; not worth a new dependency.
- **Student `co_census` (lab 3):** Small county-level pull — lab already demonstrates **both** `write_csv()` (shareable) and `saveRDS()` (R cache). That is the right dual-format lesson.
- **Lab 5 → Datawrapper:** `write_csv()` is correct — external SaaS needs interchange, not RDS.
- **Hypothetical ERN national extract (GB+):** Staff pipeline might use qs2/Parquet internally; students would still receive a course-scoped `.rds` or `.parquet` slice.

---

## Pedagogy: RDS opportunities across existing labs

| Lab | Current I/O | RDS opportunity | Suggested framing (if Tim extends) |
|-----|-------------|-----------------|--------------------------------------|
| **1** | `read.csv` (Software Carpentry) | Low priority | CSV is right for intro spreadsheets; mention RDS exists for “R objects” in passing only. |
| **2** | `get_acs()` live API | **Optional cache** | After a heavy `get_acs()` call: `saveRDS(sf_ami, "data/cache/sf_ami_2022.rds")` to teach **avoiding repeated Census API hits** (rate limits, reproducibility). Not required — adds file management. |
| **3** | `readRDS` evictions; `write_csv` + `saveRDS` for `co_census` | **Already the flagship lesson** | Strengthen contrast: CSV = “any tool can read”; RDS = “exact R object, faster reload, document in README.” |
| **4** | `readRDS` evictions; live `tigris` + joins | Optional | Could `saveRDS` merged map-ready table before `tm_shape()` if students hit slow re-runs — advanced, optional. |
| **5** | `write_csv` → Datawrapper | **Contrast moment** | Brief comment: CSV for **external tools**; `saveRDS(seg_adj)` would be for **R-only** checkpoint before mapping. |

**Already in place without new code:** Lab 3’s back-to-back `write_csv` and `saveRDS` is the natural place to discuss quick serialization vs interchange — see suggested student comment edit below.

### Suggested talking points (Tim, ~2 min in lab 3)

1. “We load ERN evictions from `.rds` because this course runs in R and we want one command, no extra packages.”
2. “When you save `co_census`, you wrote CSV **and** RDS — CSV for your teammate in Excel; RDS for you next week in R.”
3. “If you were feeding a Python team, you’d likely export **Parquet** with a data dictionary — different job than RDS.”

---

## qs vs qs2 vs RDS — official landscape

### `qs` (legacy, archived)

- **Status:** [Removed from CRAN 2026-01-17](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/qs/index.html); last release **0.27.3** (March 2025) in [CRAN Archive](https://cran.r-project.org/src/contrib/Archive/qs/).
- **Author position:** Package maintainer Travers Ching [announced deprecation in favor of `qs2`](https://github.com/qsbase/qs/issues/103) (September 2024). Reasons: CRAN policy pressure on non-public R internals, and R version updates breaking legacy serialization paths.
- **Install today:** GitHub or CRAN Archive only; **not** `install.packages("qs")`.
- **Our course files:** `d5_case_aggregated.qs` was created with this format.

### `qs2` (current CRAN successor)

- **Status:** Active on CRAN ([`qs2` 0.2.2](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/qs2/index.html), published June 2026).
- **Author position:** [Official qs vs qs2 FAQ](https://github.com/qsbase/qs2/issues/24) — `qs2` is the maintained replacement; uses approved R C API (`R_Serialize` / `R_Unserialize`) plus zstd compression and improved I/O.
- **Critical incompatibility:** **`qs2` cannot read `.qs` files** ([confirmed by author](https://github.com/qsbase/qs/issues/103#issuecomment-2777319458)). Legacy data must be loaded with `qs` once, then re-saved as `.qs2` or RDS.
- **RDS bridge:** `qs2` can convert `.qs2` ↔ `.RDS` via `qs_to_rds()` — because `qs2` wraps standard R serialization ([qs2 README](https://github.com/qsbase/qs2)). That is a reason many pipelines standardize on RDS for archival handoffs.

### RDS (base R)

- **Status:** Permanent part of R; no archival risk.
- **Best for:** Course materials, shared repos, “open this file in 10 years” reproducibility, environments where compiled dependencies are painful (DataHub, classroom hubs).
- **When to prefer compressed serializers:** Multi-GB objects, repeated load/save in HPC loops, production pipelines where staff control the image.

### Comparison (maintainer cheat sheet)

| | **RDS** | **qs (legacy)** | **qs2** |
|---|---------|-----------------|---------|
| CRAN today | Built into R | Archived | Active |
| DataHub install | Always | Compile often fails | Usually works (compiled) |
| Reads our `.qs` eviction file | No | Yes | No |
| Reads `.rds` | Yes | No | Yes (native + `qs_to_rds`) |
| Speed on huge data | Slower | Fast | Fast |
| Long-term reproducibility | Excellent | Poor (deprecated) | Good (author designed for API compliance) |

**Official recommendation from the ecosystem:** New work should use **`qs2`**, not `qs`. For **legacy `.qs` assets**, either keep a one-time `qs` 0.27.3 conversion environment or migrate to **RDS** / **qs2** and document the conversion. See [ropensci/targets#1373](https://github.com/ropensci/targets/issues/1373), [rio#473](https://github.com/gesistsa/rio/issues/473) for downstream package migrations.

---

## Why we chose RDS (not qs2 or Parquet) for SOC-N100

1. **DataHub blocker:** `qs` would not install for students; labs stopped at package setup.
2. **One-time conversion:** Staff converted `d5_case_aggregated.qs` → `.rds` once (`code/convert_qs_to_rds.R`); students inherit a zero-dependency path.
3. **Pedagogy:** Labs already teach serialization concepts; RDS keeps focus on **eviction + census analysis**, not C++ compile chains on a shared hub.
4. **qs2 mismatch:** Adopting `qs2` would still require a legacy `qs` read step for the old file — same maintainer burden, extra student package, no benefit at 7 MB.
5. **Parquet optional:** Cross-language Parquet is worthwhile for ERN distribution, not for replacing RDS in R-only labs unless `arrow` is added to the course stack.

**Future option:** If ERN ships updated eviction extracts, prefer **`.rds`** for course handouts and **`.parquet` or `.csv.gz`** for non-R distribution (document both in `data/evictions/README.md`). Do not add new `.qs` files.

---

## Lesson impact

### Lab 3 (`code/lab3_evictions.R`)

| Section | Change |
|---------|--------|
| Package header | Removed `qs` from `load_pkgs()` |
| Load Indiana evictions | `read_eviction_data()` (qs2 primary, RDS fallback) |
| Save student artifact | `saveRDS(co_census, "data/in_co_renters.rds")` |
| Teaching narrative | Still covers “save objects to disk”; now emphasizes **base R** and **documenting format choices** for reproducibility |

**Learning goals unchanged:** Join eviction counts to ACS; think about tract vs county aggregation; save intermediate objects.

### Lab 4 (`code/lab4_li_renters_mapping.R`)

| Section | Change |
|---------|--------|
| Part 3 eviction load | `read_eviction_data()` (same as lab 3) |

**Learning goals unchanged:** Map eviction patterns with `tmap`; `librarian` demo unchanged for geospatial stack.

### What students should hear (if asked)

> “We use R’s built-in `.rds` format so everyone on DataHub can load the eviction file without extra packages. When you run your own research, pick a serialization format deliberately, write it in your README, and check that collaborators can reopen your files.”

Do **not** lead with CRAN archival unless a student is curious — see inline lab comments instead.

---

## Maintainer workflow

### Regenerate `.rds` from legacy `.qs`

On a machine where `qs` 0.27.3 installs (maintainer laptop, not r.datahub):

```bash
cd SOC-N100-Housing-Precarity-2026
Rscript code/convert_qs_to_rds.R
```

Commit `data/evictions/d5_case_aggregated.rds`. See also `data/evictions/README.md`.

### Verify on DataHub

```r
file.exists("data/evictions/d5_case_aggregated.rds")
d <- readRDS("data/evictions/d5_case_aggregated.rds")
nrow(d); ncol(d)   # expect 139072 x 58
```

### CDSS / image request

We **removed `qs`** from the [DATAHUB.md](../DATAHUB.md) package request. No student-facing dependency on archived packages.

### If Tim wants qs2 in a future advanced module

- Convert source data: `qs::qread()` → `qs2::qs_save()` (maintainer-only step).
- Teach `qs_read()` / `qs_save()` with `.qs2` extension.
- Document incompatibility with legacy `.qs` in this file.

---

## Reproducibility framing (for staff teaching “data as scholarship”)

Serialization is not a neutral implementation detail — it is a **contract with your future self and collaborators**:

1. **Format** — `.csv`, `.rds`, `.qs2`, `.parquet` each imply different tools, types, and lossiness.
2. **Package availability** — archived CRAN packages break `renv`/`install.packages()` years later ([qs is the canonical 2026 example](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/qs/index.html)).
3. **Environment** — DataHub images, R version, and system libraries affect whether compiled packages install.
4. **Documentation** — Record `sessionInfo()`, file format, and conversion scripts in README or `data/README.md`.

The lab 3 inline comment models this habit for students without naming the qs crisis.

---

## References

- [CRAN: qs (archived 2026-01-17)](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/qs/index.html)
- [qs maintainer: The future of qs (#103)](https://github.com/qsbase/qs/issues/103)
- [qs2: Please read regarding qs vs qs2 (#24)](https://github.com/qsbase/qs2/issues/24)
- [qs2 package README — RDS conversion](https://github.com/qsbase/qs2)
- [CRAN: qs2](https://cran.r-project.org/web/packages/qs2/index.html)
- Course: [`code/README.md`](../code/README.md), [`DATAHUB.md`](../DATAHUB.md), [`data/evictions/README.md`](../data/evictions/README.md)

*Web research snapshot: `search-artifacts/qs-qs2-rds-serialization.json` (June 2026).*
